title: The Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, NASA's next great observatory, is finally complete
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is now complete and scheduled to launch in September. The telescope aims to reveal previously unexplored regions of the cosmos.

title: You've heard of the Space Force. Now there's a 'NASA Force' — but it's not about making space war
summary: NASA is launching a 'NASA Force' initiative to recruit industry talent for short-term roles, following the Artemis 2 moon mission. Applications are now open to bring in external expertise.

title: Bring nebulas and galaxies into focus with nearly $700 off this smart telescope
summary: The Unistellar Odyssey Pro smart telescope is being offered at nearly $700 off, making it a powerful and portable option for astrophotography despite light pollution. It allows users to capture nebulas and galaxies with ease.

title: 'Dancing' jets erupting from a cannibalistic black hole have the power of 10,000 suns
summary: Astronomers discovered jets erupting from a cannibalistic black hole with the power of 10,000 suns. This phenomenon highlights the extreme energy dynamics of such cosmic events.

title: The moon and Jupiter steal the show after sunset on April 22
summary: A half moon will pass close to Jupiter on April 22, creating a visually striking scene in the evening sky. This celestial event will be visible after sunset.

title: NASA rolls out Artemis 3 SLS rocket's huge core stage to gear up for 2027 launch (photo)
summary: NASA has rolled out the core stage of the Artemis 3 SLS rocket from the Michoud Assembly Facility to the Kennedy Space Center in Florida, preparing for a 2027 launch.

title: Astronauts photograph Lyrid meteor shower from ISS | Space photo of the day for April 21, 2026
summary: Astronaut Jessica Meir captured a photograph of the Lyrid meteor shower from the International Space Station. The image was featured as NASA's Space Photo of the Day on April 21, 2026.

title: NASA reveals its Roman Space Telescope today: How to watch live, and what's next for the next-generation observatory
summary: NASA is set to reveal the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope on April 21, with a live event available to watch on Space.com. The next-generation observatory aims to advance astronomical research and exploration.

title: NASA's Curiosity rover finds building blocks of life on Mars. Scientists aren't sure how they got there
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover discovered a variety of organic molecules on Mars, which are considered building blocks for life on Earth. Scientists are uncertain about the origin of these compounds.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4867-4872: Sand Fill In Antofagasta Crater and Finding Our Next Drill Target
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover reached the rim of the 10-meter 'Antofagasta' crater on Mars, finding it fresh and deep as expected. The rover is now searching for its next drill target within the crater.

title: NASA Invites Media to Jordan Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Jordan will sign the Artemis Accords in a ceremony hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman at NASA Headquarters in Washington on April 23. The event highlights international cooperation in space exploration under the Artemis program.

title: New NASA Views of Earth, From (S)PACE
summary: NASA utilizes a fleet of satellites to continuously observe Earth's ocean, atmosphere, and land surfaces, providing unique perspectives of our planet.

title: NASA Invests in Small Businesses Innovating for Space and Earth
summary: NASA selected over 30 companies to develop innovative technology through its SBIR and STTR programs, investing $16.3 million in seed funding. This initiative supports American industry and advances space economy solutions.

title: NASA at SXSW: Johnson Director Vanessa Wyche on Why Artemis Changes Everything
summary: NASA Johnson Space Center Director Vanessa Wyche discussed the agency's Artemis program at SXSW, highlighting its role in advancing human spaceflight from low Earth orbit to the Moon and Mars. The event emphasized NASA's preparations for a new era of space exploration.

title: NASA, Organ Sharing Network UNOS to Study Faster Organ Transport
summary: NASA's Langley Research Center is partnering with UNOS to develop faster organ transport methods using advanced aviation technologies. The collaboration aims to improve reliability in transporting donor organs for life-saving transplants.

title: What Are Ames’ Contributions to Artemis II?
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission marked the first crewed flight around the Moon in over 50 years, with Ames Research Center providing critical support for the mission. The flight is a key step toward future lunar landings under NASA's Artemis program.

title: NASA Wins Two Webby Awards, Five Webby People’s Voice Awards
summary: NASA received two Webby Awards and five Webby People's Voice Awards in the 30th Annual Webby Awards, recognizing excellence in digital media across various categories.

title: A Fresh Look at the Crab Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope released a detailed observation of the Crab Nebula on March 23, 2026, showcasing its intricate filamentary structure and evolution over 25 years. The image provides unprecedented insights into the aftermath of a supernova.

title: NASA’s Curiosity Finds Organic Molecules Never Seen Before on Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover discovered a diverse collection of organic molecules, including seven never before seen on Mars, in a rock sample analyzed in 2020. This finding highlights the potential for past or present life on the Red Planet.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to an OIG report highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the timeline and progress of the spacesuit development.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: A busy week in the space industry is expected with seven missions launching from four countries using five different launch vehicles. Notable missions include GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the New Glenn launch. This follows Blue Origin's recent achievement of landing a previously flown first-stage booster.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This marks a significant milestone in the rocket's partially reusable design.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced a new astronaut flight suit and a large docking adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This innovation supports the development of their commercial space station.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests. This marks a significant milestone for the Artemis program following the successful return of Orion.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures aboard the International Space Station in March. This marked another active month for the crew's operations.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding the CRS NG-23 mission.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. The mission marked a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration efforts.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small- and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received. The location for this achievement was not specified in the provided text.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel three to four times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved accuracy or efficiency. It highlights a specific innovation or development in this area.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using ultralight graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can control propulsion, with potential applications for steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers and graphene could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that can perform multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. The chip's versatility could enhance satellite and spacecraft systems.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, initially announced in January 2025, is an ambitious US missile defense program that has attracted significant industry interest. Companies are positioning themselves to compete for contracts related to this program.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year under its CLPS program, creating opportunities for various companies beyond just lunar-focused ones.

title: Economics of Orbital Data Centers Report: Part 1
summary: The article introduces the first part of a four-part report analyzing the economics of orbital data centers. It discusses the terrestrial bottlenecks driving the shift of compute resources to space.

title: NASA’s 2028 Moon Landing Has A Spacesuit Problem, New Report Says
summary: NASA's planned 2028 Moon landing faces delays due to spacesuit development issues, according to a new report from the Office of the Inspector General. The report highlights a significant challenge in meeting the mission timeline.

title: Latvia To Join Artemis Accords Today
summary: Latvia is set to join the Artemis Accords today, marking another signatory to the international agreement. Redwire's president emphasized the need for countries to move from supporting norms to contributing to the Artemis program.

title: SpaceX Won A Mars Mission That Might Get Cancelled
summary: SpaceX has been awarded a contract to launch the ESA's Rosalind Franklin rover to Mars by late 2028 for $175.7M, but NASA's potential cancellation of its participation in the mission due to budget constraints leaves its future uncertain.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is expanding into the thruster market, aiming to vertically integrate production for scale. The company's CFO highlighted concerns about relying on small suppliers for critical components.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is preparing its first qualification spacesuit for a 2027 flight, as confirmed by Chief Astronaut Michael López-Alegría. The development is ongoing despite external uncertainties.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS). Axiom Space previously dominated NASA's PAM contracts but now faces new competition in 2026.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has established a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulation, national security, and international collaboration.

title: Pentagon to Move Forward On AEP Upgrades for GPS, As DoD Cancels GPS OCX
summary: The Pentagon will cancel the GPS Next Generation Operational Control System (OCX) developed by RTX and instead proceed with upgrades to the existing ground system for GPS. The decision involves moving forward with Architecture Evolution Plan (AEP) upgrades, as reported by Lockheed Martin.

title: Intellian Details New Terminals for Aviation and WGS Network
summary: Intellian Technologies announced two new satellite terminals, including its first aviation terminal and a new tactical flyaway terminal. The company is expanding its offerings in the satellite technology sector.

title: Eutelsat Retires GEO Satellite 139 West A to Graveyard Orbit
summary: Eutelsat has retired its GEO communications satellite Eutelsat 139 West A after 22 years of service. The satellite was moved to a graveyard orbit following the completion of its mission.

title: Air India Chief Digital Officer Talks IFC Roadmap at AIX: Video Interview
summary: Air India's Chief Digital and Technology Officer discussed the airline's In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) roadmap at the Aircraft Interiors Expo (AIX) in Hamburg. The interview was conducted by Via Satellite's Senior Editorial Director Mark Holmes.

title: Airline, OEM, Satellite Players Say IFC Experience is in the ‘Details’
summary: Airlines, OEMs, and satellite providers are collaborating to improve in-flight connectivity (IFC) by addressing detailed challenges to meet rising customer expectations. Industry leaders discussed these efforts at the Aircraft Interiors (AIX) event.

title: Poland Invests in New Defense GEO Satellite Built by Thales, Airbus, and Radmor
summary: Poland has signed a contract with Thales, Airbus, and Radmor to develop a new Geostationary Orbit (GEO) defense satellite. This investment strengthens Poland's space defense capabilities.

title: New Glenn Grounded and AST SpaceMobile Satellite Lost After Launch Anomaly
summary: New Glenn rocket is grounded after a launch anomaly caused AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite to be placed in the wrong orbit, leading to its loss. AST SpaceMobile plans to file an insurance claim for the failed satellite.

title: Ovzon Launches New Mobile Terminal in Small Form Factor
summary: Ovzon, a Swedish satellite operator, has launched a new mobile satellite terminal called the Ovzon T8 in a small form factor. The terminal is designed for mobility across air, land, and sea.

title: Inside SatEnlight’s Spiral Staircase of Lasers: Interview with Startup Space Winner Matteo Vismara
summary: SatEnlight, an Italian optical communications company based in Milan, uses a unique multi-layered laser beam arrangement for its technology. The company's innovation was highlighted in an interview with its founder Matteo Vismara, a Startup Space winner.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo highlighted satellite companies advocating for in-flight connectivity (IFC) solutions to airlines. Via Satellite reported on key announcements in this sector.
